skill.name: ask-matt
skill.description: Choose the right Matt Pocock skill or workflow for a coding task, from idea refinement through implementation.
skill.summary: Routes users to the correct Matt Pocock skill flow for engineering work.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, one documentation URL, and weak-crypto keywords. Review found no executable code, no network calls, no cryptographic implementation, and no prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the backticks only format slash-command names in documentation. No executable script or shell invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the flagged text is inline formatting around command names. There is no Ruby, shell script, or command substitution syntax to execute.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Link Only
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported a hardcoded URL, but the URL is a normal Markdown reference link. The skill does not fetch, post, or transmit data to that address.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside prose as a reference link about context limits. No code path performs a network request or data transfer.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators, but the referenced lines contain prose and skill metadata. No cryptographic function, hash, cipher, or credential handling appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines describe the skill and a stateless interview flow. They do not implement or recommend any cryptographic algorithm.

content.user_title: Choose the Right Matt Pocock Skill
content.value_statement: Users can waste time choosing the wrong engineering workflow. This skill routes a task to the right Matt Pocock skill or flow.
actual_capabilities: Explains the main idea-to-shipping workflow across related skills.
Routes codebase ideas to grilling, PRD creation, issue splitting, and implementation steps.
Identifies when a prototype or handoff session should be used.
Distinguishes raw issue triage from already prepared implementation issues.
Points users to setup, teaching, writing, and architecture maintenance flows.
limitations: It does not implement code or run tools by itself.
It assumes the related Matt Pocock skills are installed and available.
It is a routing guide, not a general project management system.
It does not verify issue tracker setup or repository state.

use_cases.title: Start a New Feature Flow
use_cases.description: Decide whether to begin with grilling, a prototype, a PRD, issue creation, or direct implementation.

use_cases.title: Handle Incoming Bugs
use_cases.description: Route raw bug reports and feature requests through triage before assigning implementation work.

use_cases.title: Preserve Context Across Sessions
use_cases.description: Choose between handoff and compacting when a session becomes full or needs a branch.

prompt_templates.title: Pick My Starting Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner routing for a new task
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a coding task and I am not sure where to start. Ask me the minimum questions needed and recommend the first Matt Pocock skill to use.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an Idea-to-Ship Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn a feature idea into a skill sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Map this feature idea to the Matt Pocock workflow. Tell me when to use grilling, a PRD, issue splitting, and implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Handoff or Compact
prompt_templates.scenario: Manage a long or branching AI session
prompt_templates.prompt: My current session is getting large and I may need a prototype branch. Recommend whether to use handoff or compact, and explain the next step.

prompt_templates.title: Audit My Workflow Choice
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned sequence of Matt Pocock skills for this project. Identify misplaced steps, missing setup, and context risks before I continue.

output_examples.input: I have a vague product idea in an existing repository.
output: Start with grill-with-docs because the idea depends on a codebase.
Stay in one context window while the idea becomes a PRD and issues.
Use implement in fresh sessions for each independent issue.

output_examples.input: I need to explore a UI behavior before writing the full plan.
output: Use handoff to preserve the current thread before branching.
Run a prototype session to answer the runnable question.
Use handoff again to bring the result back into the planning thread.

output_examples.input: My repository has many raw bug reports from users.
output: Use triage for incoming reports that are not yet agent-ready.
Do not triage issues already produced by the issue-splitting workflow.
After triage, assign implementation work one issue at a time.

best_practices: Run setup before the first engineering flow so issue labels and docs match the other skills.
Keep grilling, PRD creation, and issue splitting in one continuous context when possible.
Start a fresh implementation session for each independent issue from a multi-session plan.
anti_patterns: Do not use triage on issues already generated from a PRD.
Do not compact in the middle of a phase that needs detailed prior context.
Do not push a long planning session past useful context limits when handoff is available.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps choose the right Matt Pocock skill or workflow for an engineering situation.

faq.question: Does it write code?
faq.answer: No. It routes you to skills that plan, prototype, triage, or implement work.

faq.question: When should I use grill-with-docs?
faq.answer: Use it when you have an idea that depends on an existing codebase.

faq.question: When should I use grill-me?
faq.answer: Use it when there is no codebase and you want to sharpen a plan or design.

faq.question: What is the difference between handoff and compact?
faq.answer: Handoff preserves context in a file for a fresh session. Compact continues in the same conversation with a summary.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found only Markdown guidance, with no executable commands, network calls, or prompt injection.
